description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a military olive-green color scheme and a red star insignia on the tail. The aircraft features two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, supported by black struts, and a single propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is boxy and angular, with a fixed, exposed landing gear consisting of two main wheels and a tail skid. The cockpit is positioned just behind the engine, appearing to be open and likely designed for a single pilot. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with simple, flat surfaces. The overall design suggests a utilitarian, early 20th-century aircraft suitable for display or thematic use in an urban park or museum setting.
description: A small, olive-green biplane with stacked wings, a single propeller, and a red star insignia on the tail. It features an open cockpit, fixed landing gear, and a boxy fuselage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stacked biplane wings, central fuselage, and the propeller aligned with the nose.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the boxy fuselage, exposed engine, single main wheel, and tail skid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, stacked wings, and the propeller extending forward.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the red star insignia on the tail, cockpit placement, and landing gear details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engine, likely single-seat.
